items.summary: This pull request introduces a significant technical update to the openclaw project by adding support for a CLI provider in the text-to-speech (TTS) module. Previously, users relied solely on cloud-based TTS services, which posed privacy concerns and increased latency. The update enables local CLI TTS tools (such as espeak, piper, mlx-audio) to be used directly, enhancing privacy by keeping data on-device and allowing offline operation. The implementation includes support for template variables, environment variable passing, and improved configuration flexibility. This change addresses issues related to data privacy and performance, representing a notable bug fix and feature enhancement that optimizes TTS functionality within the platform.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a bug in the openclaw/openclaw project where the `Edit` tool unexpectedly deletes file content, resulting in a 0-byte file, despite indicating success. This problem occurs during in-place text replacements: although the tool correctly identifies the old text, it fails to perform the replacement properly, leading to complete file loss. The issue is reproducible across specific environments, including Docker sandbox on Linux with an ARM64 architecture, using version v2026.3.11. The bug impacts file editing operations, potentially affecting code integrity and version control. No specific fix or performance optimization details are provided in the report.

items.title: [Bug]: isolated cron job sessions with kimi-coding/k2p5 as a model can't use tools · Issue #44269 · openclaw/openclaw
highlights:
44269 - Repository: openclaw/openclaw | Your own personal AI assistant. Any OS. Any Platform. The lobster way. 🦞 | 308K stars | TypeScript ## [Bug]: isolated cron job sessions with kimi-coding/k2p5 as a model can't use tools - Author: @TchelloSimis
- State: open
- Labels: bug, regression
- Created: 2026-03-12T17:28:47Z - Updated: 2026-03-13T00:54:12Z ### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary Ever since 2026.3.2, isolated cron job sessions with kimi-coding/k2p5 as a model can't use tools. This means it can't access thew workspace and do any types of tasks. It seems to be exclusive to Kimi models. The issue perisists even in more recent releases. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a mock text file at /home/clawdbot/.openclaw/workspace/. I created a file called fruits.txt, with contents:  ...    `openclaw cron create --name "test-fruit" --at "+3m" --model kimi-coding/k2p5 --session isolated --channel telegram --to "CHAT_ID" --message "Read the contents of /home/clawdbot/.openclaw/workspace/fruit.txt and output the full  ...    1. Wait for execution

### Expected behavior

Agent outputs

```
mango
pineapple
kiwi
pomegranate
```  ...    ```
apple
banana
cherry
dragonfruit
elderberry
```

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.11

### Operating system Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

_No response_

### Model

kimi-coding/k2p5

### Provider / routing chain openclaw -> kimi-coding -> kimi

### Config file / key location

_No response_  ...    ### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

items.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression introduced in openclaw version 2026.3.2, where isolated cron job sessions using the kimi-coding/k2p5 model can no longer access tools or the workspace. Despite previous functionality, these sessions are now unable to perform tasks such as reading files or interacting with the environment, specifically affecting Kimi models. The issue persists across recent releases and is demonstrated through a reproducible example involving a cron job attempting to read a text file. This problem hampers automation and tool integration for Kimi-based models within openclaw.

items.title: macOS Release - OpenClaw
highlights:
OpenClaw macOS release (Sparkle) This app now ships Sparkle auto-updates. Release builds must be Developer ID–signed, zipped, and published with a  ...    ## Prereqs - Sparkle tools are fetched automatically via SwiftPM at `apps/macos/.build/artifacts/sparkle/Sparkle/bin/`  ...    - `xcrun notarytool store-credentials "openclaw-notary" --key /tmp/openclaw-notary.p8 --key-id  ...    - `echo "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_P8" | sed's/\\n/\n/g' > /tmp/openclaw-notary.p8` - `APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_P8`, `APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_ID`, `APP_STORE_CONNECT_ISSUER_ID` - We use a Keychain profile named `openclaw-notary`, created from App Store Connect API key env vars in your shell  ...    - Use `scripts/package-mac-dist.sh` for release artifacts (zip + DMG + notarization). Use `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` for local/dev packaging. - For `BUILD_CONFIG=release`, `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` now defaults to universal (`arm64 x86_64`) automatically. You can still override with `BUILD_ARCHS=arm64` or `BUILD_ARCHS=x86_64`. For local/dev builds (`BUILD_CONFIG=debug`), it defaults to the current architecture (`$(uname -m)`). - You can still override `APP_BUILD` explicitly when release engineering needs a specific monotonic value.  ...    (`YYYYMMDDNN`: stable defaults to `90`, prereleases use a suffix-derived lane) and uses the higher of that value and  ...    ```bash
# From repo root; set release IDs so Sparkle feed is enabled.  ...    # APP_BUILD must be numeric + monotonic for Sparkle compare.
# Default is auto-derived from APP_VERSION when omitted. SKIP_NOTARIZE=1 \
BUNDLE_ID=ai.openclaw.mac \
APP_VERSION=2026.3.11 \
BUILD_CONFIG=release \ SIGN_IDENTITY="Developer ID Application: <Developer Name> (<TEAMID>)" \
scripts/package-mac-dist.sh # `package-mac-dist.sh` already creates the zip + DMG. # If you used `package-mac-app.sh` directly instead, create them manually:  ...    ditto -c -k --sequesterRsrc --keepParent dist/OpenClaw.app dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip  ...    scripts/create-dmg.sh dist/OpenClaw.app dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.dmg  ...    #   xcrun notarytool store-credentials "openclaw-notary" \  ...    NOTARIZE=1 NOTARYTOOL_PROFILE=openclaw-notary \
BUNDLE_ID=ai.openclaw.mac \
APP_VERSION=2026.3.11 \  ...    ditto -c -k --keepParent apps/macos/.build/release/OpenClaw.app.dSYM dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.dSYM.zip ```

## Appcast entry

Use the release note generator so Sparkle renders formatted HTML notes: ```bash SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE=/path/to/ed25519-private-key scripts/make_appcast.sh dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml ``` Generates HTML release notes from `CHANGELOG.md` (via `scripts/changelog-to-html.sh`) and embeds them in the appcast entry. Commit the updated `appcast.xml` alongside the release assets (zip + dSYM) when publishing. ## Publish & verify - On a previous public build, run “Check for Updates…” from the About tab and verify Sparkle installs the new build cleanly. - `curl -I ` returns 200 after assets upload. - `curl -I https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml` returns 200.
- Sanity checks:  ...    `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml`. - Upload `OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip` (and `OpenClaw-2026.3.11.dSYM.zip`) to the GitHub release for tag `v2026.3.11`. Definition of done: signed app
items.summary: The webpage provides documentation for the macOS release of OpenClaw, focusing on its integration with Sparkle for auto-updates. It details prerequisites such as fetching Sparkle tools via SwiftPM, installing dependencies with pnpm, and configuring notarization with Apple credentials. The release process involves signing builds with Developer ID, creating signed appcast entries, and publishing updates. For comprehensive API details, architecture specifics, release notes, and changelog, users are directed to the full documentation index at [https://docs.openclaw.ai/llms.txt](https://docs.openclaw.ai/llms.txt).

items.summary: This pull request addresses a bug in the OpenClaw project related to OAuth profile expiry display. Previously, the system incorrectly showed stale expiry information ("expires in 0m") for auto-renewable OAuth profiles, despite the status being overridden to "ok" after a refresh. The fix involves clearing the `expiresAt` and `remainingMs` fields when profiles are set to auto-renew, ensuring that downstream renderers accurately reflect the profile's current state without displaying misleading expiry times. This update improves the correctness of OAuth profile status indicators, preventing confusion caused by outdated expiry data.

items.summary: This pull request addresses a bug related to the Responses API in the openclaw project. It enables sanitization of tool call IDs for non-OpenAI providers to ensure they conform to the 64-character limit, preventing potential issues with ID handling. OpenAI's own IDs remain unchanged to preserve their canonical format. The update improves compatibility and robustness when integrating custom proxies with the Responses API, especially for non-OpenAI services.

items.title: [Bug]: Cron jobs enqueued but never execute — lane never dispatches, runningAtMs written on enqueue causes permanent stale marker loop · Issue #42960 · openclaw/openclaw
highlights:
openclaw/openclaw #42960 - Repository: openclaw/openclaw | Your own personal AI assistant. Any OS. Any Platform. The lobster way. 🦞 | 305K stars | TypeScript ## [Bug]: Cron jobs enqueued but never execute — lane never dispatches, runningAtMs written on enqueue causes permanent stale marker loop - Author: @Captain-Scarlet
- State: open (reopened)
- Labels: bug, regression - Created: 2026-03-11T07:54:25Z
- Updated: 2026-03-12T03:42:45Z
- Closed by: @frankekn ### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary Cron jobs return enqueued: true from both scheduled triggers and openclaw cron run but never actually execute — no session start, no LLM call, no tool activity appears in logs. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Have one or more cron jobs configured in jobs.json with enabled: true
2. Run: openclaw cron run  ...    5. Run: openclaw logs --max-bytes 50000 | tail -40  ...    The job is enqueued and runningAtMs is immediately written to jobs.json. The cron lane never dispatches it. No session is created, no LLM call is made. On the next gateway restart, OpenClaw logs "cron: clearing stale running marker on  ...    ### OpenClaw version

2026.3.8 (3caab92)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04 LTS

### Install method npm install -g openclaw@latest — running as systemd service via openclaw-gateway.service

### Model vllm/Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B (self-hosted via vLLM on RunPod, OpenAI-compatible endpoint)

### Provider / routing chain vllm provider → https:// -8000.proxy.runpod.net/v1 → vLLM serving Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B

### Config file / key location  ...    Provider defined with "api": "openai-completions", "contextWindow": 131072, "maxTokens": 16000. Agent smith-vciso uses "model": "vllm/Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B". The same model works correctly for interactive chat sessions — only cron execution is broken. The issue reproduces with any cron job regardless of which agent or model is configured.  ...    ### Impact and severity Affected: All cron jobs across all agents — 100% failure rate Severity: Blocks workflow entirely — the entire scheduled automation layer is non-functional  ...    persists across gateway restarts, runs/ directory deletion, and manual runningAtMs removal from jobs.json. Previously working cron jobs stopped executing after a period of LLM timeouts (RunPod pod was stopped while cron jobs were  ...    ---

### Timeline

**Captain-Scarlet** added label `bug` · Mar 11, 2026 at 7:54am **openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `regression` · Mar 11, 2026 at 7:54am **@shuicici** commented · Mar 11, 2026 at 8:24am > I reproduced/investigated this from source and the symptom matches a lane deadlock that was fixed on `main`.
> > ## Likely root cause
> > Isolated cron runs were able to reuse the `cron` command lane for the nested embedded-agent call, which can deadlock: > 
> - outer cron task holds `cron` lane
> - inner embedded run waits to reacquire the same lane > - result: `cron run` returns `enqueued: true`, `runningAtMs` is persisted, but no session/tool/LLM activity ever  ...    > - `src/cron/service.issue-regressions.test.ts -t "#17554"` ✅
> - `src/cron/service.armtimer-tight-loop.test.ts` ✅  ...    **SergioChan** mentioned this in PR #43055: fix(cron): avoid manual run self-deadlock on cron lane · Mar  ...    **frankekn** closed this · Mar 12, 2026 at 2
items.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression in the openclaw/openclaw project where cron jobs are enqueued successfully but fail to execute. Despite returning "enqueued: true" during manual runs, no session activity, language model calls, or tool actions occur, indicating a failure in dispatching the scheduled tasks. The problem appears linked to a "stale marker loop" caused by the `runningAtMs` timestamp written at enqueue time. The issue was reopened and remains unresolved, affecting the reliability of scheduled jobs in the system.

items.title: [Bug]: openai-responses fails with "Invalid input[n].id: string too long" after tool calls / session startup · Issue #43305 · openclaw/openclaw
highlights:
43305  ...    | TypeScript ## [Bug]: openai-responses fails with "Invalid input[n].id: string too long" after tool calls / session startup - Author: @adirgan
- State: open
- Labels: bug, regression
- Created: 2026-03-11T15:45:20Z - Updated: 2026-03-12T00:25:34Z ### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary When OpenClaw is configured with api: "openai-responses" against an OpenAI-compatible proxy, some sessions fail immediately after tool calls or session startup with HTTP 400: Invalid 'input[n].id': string too long.... Switching the same provider to api: "openai-completions" avoids the failure. ### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure a custom provider in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json using:  ...    • 400 Invalid 'input[3].id': string too long. Expected a string with maximum length 64, but got a string with length  ...    ### Actual behavior OpenClaw sends at least one input[n].id longer than 64 chars in the responses payload, causing the backend to reject the request and the assistant to stop responding for that turn/session.  ...    • OpenClaw version: 2026.2.26 (from config metadata)
• Runtime model observed: lanproxy/gpt-5.4 • Provider: custom OpenAI-compatible proxy (lanproxy)
• API mode that fails: openai-responses  ...    ### OpenClaw version

2026.3.8 (build 3caab92)

### Operating system

Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 (Ubuntu)  ...    Custom provider configured in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json as "lanproxy". Provider uses an OpenAI-compatible endpoint at http://10.20.30.95:8317/v1. Failure occurs when provider API mode is set to "openai-responses". Same setup works when switching the provider API mode to "openai-completions".  ...    - HTTP 400: Invalid 'input[3].id': string too long. Expected a string with maximum length 64, but got a string with  ...    Affected: OpenClaw users using a custom OpenAI-compatible provider configured with api="openai-responses"; observed in  ...    ### Additional information

Important notes: - This does not appear to be caused by Lobster. - A separate warning about lobster allowlist entries was present, but the reproducible failure is the responses payload validation error. - The issue appears specific to the openai-responses path. - The same provider, model, and environment work when switching the provider API mode to "openai-completions". - This suggests a bug in how OpenClaw serializes or sanitizes IDs for the Responses API path. ---

### Timeline

**adirgan** added label `bug` · Mar 11, 2026 at 3:45pm **openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `regression` · Mar 11, 2026 at 3:58pm **kincoy** mentioned this in PR [#43315: fix(models): enable tool call ID sanitization for openai-responses  ...    > Working on a fix for this. Will have a PR up shortly.
> 
> — Joel Nishanth · offlyn.AI  ...    > Fix tracked in #43545. Tool
items.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression in the openclaw/openclaw project where sessions fail with a "Invalid input[n].id: string too long" error after tool calls or startup when configured with `api: "openai-responses"` and an OpenAI-compatible proxy. The failure results in an HTTP 400 error, but switching the API setting to `openai-completions` prevents the issue. The problem occurs when using custom providers with specific configurations, indicating a bug introduced in recent updates.

items.summary: The webpage discusses an issue with the versioning rules of the openclaw plugin used in WeChat Work (企业微信). The user highlights a problem where the plugin's prerelease version (1.0.7-beta.1) is not installed by default, as npm requires explicit request for prereleases. This misconfiguration leads to difficulties in deploying the latest beta versions. The discussion points to a GitHub issue where this version resolution behavior is documented, emphasizing the need for proper version specification to ensure correct plugin installation. The content is relevant for developers involved in plugin management, version control, and deployment strategies, especially when dealing with prerelease versions in a production environment.
